  tags: [political-philosophy, censorship, education, nonfiction]

- id: propaganda
  title: Propaganda
  author: Edward Bernays
  original_year: 1928
  original_language: English
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: An early account of organized opinion management, symbols, authority, and mass persuasion.
  why_included: Helps readers identify techniques that governments and institutions use to shape public belief.
  tags: [propaganda, media-literacy, psychology, nonfiction]

- id: public-opinion
  title: Public Opinion
  author: Walter Lippmann
  original_year: 1922
  original_language: English
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: An examination of stereotypes, media, expertise, and the construction of public belief.
  why_included: Gives readers tools for recognizing manufactured pictures of reality.
  tags: [propaganda, media-literacy, psychology, nonfiction]

- id: manual-for-the-solution-of-military-ciphers
  title: Manual for the Solution of Military Ciphers
  author: Parker Hitt
  original_year: 1916
  original_language: English
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: A systematic historical guide to analyzing substitution and transposition ciphers.
  why_included: Preserves a practical foundation in private communication and cryptanalysis.
  tags: [cryptography, communications, mathematics, nonfiction]

- id: elementary-cryptanalysis
  title: Elementary Cryptanalysis
  author: Helen Fouché Gaines
  original_year: 1939
  original_language: English
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]

  tags: [cryptography, communications, mathematics, nonfiction]

- id: the-federalist-papers
  title: The Federalist Papers
  author: Alexander Hamilton, James Madison, and John Jay
  original_year: 1787
  original_language: English
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: Essays on divided power, representation, faction, and constitutional government.
  why_included: A primary-source reference on checks and balances and concentrated authority.
  tags: [constitutionalism, civics, rights, nonfiction]

- id: the-subjection-of-women
  title: The Subjection of Women
  author: John Stuart Mill
  original_year: 1869
  original_language: English
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: An argument against the legal and social subordination of women.
  why_included: Direct challenge to gender hierarchy and coercive custom.
  tags: [gender, equality, rights, nonfiction]

- id: what-is-property
  title: What Is Property?
  original_title: Qu'est-ce que la propriété?
  author: Pierre-Joseph Proudhon
  original_year: 1840
  original_language: French
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge, original-language]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: A critique of conventional property rights and their relationship to power.
  why_included: Politically controversial challenge to ownership, hierarchy, and the state.
  tags: [economics, property, politics, nonfiction]

- id: capital-volume-i
  title: Capital, Volume I
  author: Karl Marx
  original_year: 1867
  original_language: German
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge, original-language]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  english_availability: edition-review-required
  description: A critique of capital, labor, ownership, exploitation, and class domination.
  why_included: Repeatedly banned or restricted under anticommunist and authoritarian governments.
  tags: [economics, labor, politics, nonfiction]

- id: democracy-in-america
  title: Democracy in America
  author: Alexis de Tocqueville
  original_year: 1835
  original_language: French
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge, original-language]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: An examination of democracy, civic associations, conformity, majority power, and soft despotism.
  why_included: Major analysis of democratic strengths and vulnerabilities.
  tags: [democracy, civics, politics, nonfiction]

- id: the-age-of-reason
  title: The Age of Reason
  author: Thomas Paine
  original_year: 1794
  original_language: English
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: A critique of revealed religion and scriptural authority.
  why_included: Essential freethought text frequently condemned for its challenge to religious power.
  tags: [freethought, religion, skepticism, nonfiction]

- id: dialogues-concerning-natural-religion
  title: Dialogues Concerning Natural Religion
  author: David Hume
  original_year: 1779
  original_language: English
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: Philosophical dialogue examining arguments about God, design, and religious belief.
  why_included: Durable model of rigorous skepticism about religious authority.
  tags: [freethought, religion, philosophy, nonfiction]

- id: the-life-of-jesus-critically-examined
  title: The Life of Jesus Critically Examined
  author: David Friedrich Strauss
  original_year: 1835
  original_language: German
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge, original-language]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  english_availability: edition-review-required
  description: Historical criticism of Gospel narratives and their interpretation.
  why_included: Significant challenge to orthodox scriptural authority.
  tags: [religion, historical-criticism, freethought, nonfiction]

- id: the-essence-of-christianity
  title: The Essence of Christianity
  author: Ludwig Feuerbach
  original_year: 1841
  original_language: German
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge, original-language]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: Interprets religion as a projection of human qualities and needs.
  why_included: Influential critique of theological authority and religious anthropology.
  tags: [religion, philosophy, freethought, nonfiction]

- id: why-i-am-an-agnostic
  title: Why I Am an Agnostic
  author: Robert G. Ingersoll
  original_year: 1896
  original_language: English
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: An accessible defense of agnosticism and intellectual freedom.
  why_included: Representative American freethought writing challenging clerical authority.
  tags: [agnosticism, freethought, religion, nonfiction]

- id: the-psychology-of-suggestion
  title: The Psychology of Suggestion
  author: Boris Sidis

  original_language: English
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: Early study of suggestibility, imitation, hypnosis, and mental influence.
  why_included: Historical context for propaganda, persuasion, and coercive environments.

- id: the-crowd
  title: The Crowd

  original_language: French
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge, original-language]
  rights_status: historical-work

- id: instincts-of-the-herd-in-peace-and-war

source_documents.world-literature.yaml:
# Broad canon and censorship inventory. Each pre-1931 label refers to the underlying work;
# a particular English translation or scan still needs edition-specific review.
- id: madame-bovary
  title: Madame Bovary
  author: Gustave Flaubert
  original_year: 1857
  original_language: French
  collections: [banned-challenged, essential-literature, original-language]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: A provincial woman seeks escape from marriage and convention through romance, consumption, and fantasy.
  why_included: Flaubert was prosecuted for obscenity and offenses against public morality.
  tags: [literary-canon, sexuality, censorship, fiction]

- id: crime-and-punishment
  title: Crime and Punishment
  original_title: Prestupleniye i nakazaniye
  author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
  original_year: 1866
  original_language: Russian
  collections: [banned-challenged, essential-literature, original-language, suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: An impoverished student commits murder while testing whether he stands beyond ordinary morality.
  why_included: A central world novel about ideology, poverty, conscience, policing, religion, and moral autonomy.
  tags: [literary-canon, crime, philosophy, fiction]

- id: anna-karenina
  title: Anna Karenina
  author: Leo Tolstoy
  original_year: 1877
  original_language: Russian
  collections: [banned-challenged, essential-literature, original-language]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: Interlocking stories of marriage, adultery, family, faith, and social judgment in imperial Russia.
  why_included: Frequently ranked among the greatest novels and controversial for sexuality, suicide, religion, and social convention.
  tags: [literary-canon, marriage, religion, fiction]

- id: war-and-peace
  title: War and Peace
  author: Leo Tolstoy
  original_year: 1869
  original_language: Russian
  collections: [essential-literature, original-language, suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: Families experience love, war, social upheaval, and historical change during the Napoleonic invasion of Russia.
  why_included: A perennial top-ranked novel with political, religious, pacifist, and historical significance.
  tags: [literary-canon, war, history, fiction]

- id: the-brothers-karamazov
  title: The Brothers Karamazov
  author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
  original_year: 1880
  original_language: Russian
  collections: [essential-literature, original-language, suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: A family drama of patricide, faith, doubt, freedom, guilt, and moral responsibility.
  why_included: Commonly ranked among the greatest novels and repeatedly controversial for theology and political implications.
  tags: [literary-canon, religion, philosophy, fiction]

- id: les-miserables
  title: Les Misérables
  author: Victor Hugo
  original_year: 1862
  original_language: French
  collections: [essential-literature, original-language, suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: An ex-convict's pursuit of redemption unfolds amid poverty, revolution, law, and social injustice.
  why_included: Restricted or condemned for revolutionary politics, anticlericalism, and criticism of social institutions.
  tags: [literary-canon, law, revolution, fiction]

- id: the-divine-comedy
  title: The Divine Comedy
  author: Dante Alighieri
  original_year: 1321
  original_language: Italian
  collections: [banned-challenged, essential-literature, original-language]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: A journey through Hell, Purgatory, and Paradise combining theology, politics, poetry, and personal judgment.
  why_included: A foundational work subject to religious objection, bowdlerization, and political controversy.
  tags: [literary-canon, poetry, religion, politics]

- id: the-iliad
  title: The Iliad
  author: Homer
  original_year: -750
  original_language: Ancient Greek
  collections: [banned-challenged, essential-literature, original-language]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: An epic of rage, honor, mortality, and warfare during the Trojan War.
  why_included: Foundational literature challenged for violence, sexuality, pagan religion, and classroom suitability.
  tags: [literary-canon, epic, war, poetry]

- id: the-odyssey
  title: The Odyssey
  author: Homer
  original_year: -700
  original_language: Ancient Greek
  collections: [banned-challenged, essential-literature, original-language]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: Odysseus struggles to return home through war, temptation, monsters, and divine interference.
  why_included: Enduring canonical work challenged for violence, sexuality, paganism, and depictions of slavery.
  tags: [literary-canon, epic, travel, poetry]

- id: fathers-and-sons
  title: Fathers and Sons
  author: Ivan Turgenev
  original_year: 1862
  original_language: Russian
  collections: [essential-literature, original-language, suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: A young nihilist confronts family, tradition, love, and the limits of radical detachment.
  why_included: Politically controversial for nihilism, generational rebellion, and revolutionary associations.
  tags: [politics, family, literary-canon, fiction]

- id: dead-souls
  title: Dead Souls
  author: Nikolai Gogol
  original_year: 1842
  original_language: Russian
  collections: [essential-literature, original-language, suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: A confidence man purchases the legal identities of dead serfs in a satire of bureaucracy and corruption.
  why_included: Censored before publication and later politically managed for its attacks on Russian society.
  tags: [satire, bureaucracy, serfdom, fiction]

- id: notes-from-underground
  title: Notes from Underground
  author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
  original_year: 1864
  original_language: Russian
  collections: [essential-literature, original-language, suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: An isolated narrator attacks rational utopianism while exposing resentment and self-destruction.
  why_included: A foundational existential work with political, religious, and anti-utopian significance.
  tags: [philosophy, psychology, politics, fiction]

- id: demons
  title: Demons
  original_title: Besy
  author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
  original_year: 1872
  original_language: Russian
  collections: [essential-literature, original-language, suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: Political radicals, manipulators, and idealists become entangled in conspiracy and murder.
  why_included: Important for revolutionary politics, terrorism, atheism, religion, and extremism.
  tags: [politics, extremism, religion, fiction]

- id: the-sorrows-of-young-werther
  title: The Sorrows of Young Werther
  author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
  original_year: 1774
  original_language: German
  collections: [banned-challenged, essential-literature, original-language]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: A sensitive young man becomes consumed by impossible love and despair.
  why_included: Banned in several places amid fears that its treatment of suicide would encourage imitation.
  tags: [suicide, romance, literary-canon, fiction]

- id: dangerous-liaisons
  title: Dangerous Liaisons
  author: Pierre Choderlos de Laclos
  original_year: 1782
  original_language: French
  collections: [banned-challenged, essential-literature, original-language]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: Aristocratic libertines manipulate lovers and rivals through seduction and correspondence.
  why_included: Repeatedly restricted for sexual exploitation, adultery, and moral corruption.
  tags: [sexuality, letters, aristocracy, fiction]

- id: tartuffe
  title: Tartuffe
  author: Molière
  original_year: 1664
  original_language: French
  collections: [banned-challenged, essential-literature, original-language, suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: A religious impostor gains control over a wealthy household through false piety.
  why_included: Banned from public performance after religious authorities objected to its attack on hypocrisy.
  tags: [religion, satire, theater, censorship]

- id: emile
  title: Émile, or On Education
  author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau
  original_year: 1762
  original_language: French
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge, original-language]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: A philosophical account of education, human development, nature, society, and belief.
  why_included: Burned and banned for its religious arguments and challenge to established authority.
  tags: [education, religion, political-philosophy, nonfiction]

- id: the-theological-political-treatise
  title: Theological-Political Treatise
  author: Baruch Spinoza
  original_year: 1670
  original_language: Latin
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge, original-language]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: An argument for freedom of thought, historical reading of scripture, and limits on religious authority.
  why_included: Formally banned and denounced for biblical criticism and defense of free expression.
  tags: [free-expression, religion, philosophy, censorship]

- id: the-count-of-monte-cristo
  title: The Count of Monte Cristo
  author: Alexandre Dumas
  original_year: 1844
  original_language: French
  collections: [banned-challenged, essential-literature, original-language]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: A falsely imprisoned man acquires wealth and remakes himself as an instrument of revenge.
  why_included: Frequently abridged or sanitized for violence, sexuality, drugs, suicide, and political material.
  tags: [revenge, adventure, literary-canon, fiction]

- id: the-three-musketeers
  title: The Three Musketeers
  author: Alexandre Dumas
  original_year: 1844
  original_language: French
  collections: [essential-literature, original-language]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: Four companions navigate warfare, court politics, romance, betrayal, and intrigue.
  why_included: Often abridged for sexuality, adultery, violence, and political intrigue.
  tags: [adventure, politics, friendship, fiction]

- id: middlemarch
  title: Middlemarch
  author: George Eliot
  original_year: 1871
  original_language: English
  collections: [essential-literature, banned-challenged]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: Interconnected lives expose ambition, marriage, medicine, politics, money, and social limitation.
  why_included: Major English novel criticized for adultery, religious doubt, gender roles, and social criticism.
  tags: [literary-canon, marriage, religion, fiction]

- id: germinal
  title: Germinal
  author: Émile Zola
  original_year: 1885
  original_language: French
  collections: [banned-challenged, original-language, suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: Coal miners confront exploitation, hunger, political division, and violent labor conflict.
  why_included: Restricted for socialism, labor militancy, sexuality, violence, and attacks on industrial capitalism.
  tags: [labor, socialism, class, fiction]

- id: the-kreutzer-sonata
  title: The Kreutzer Sonata
  author: Leo Tolstoy
  original_year: 1890
  original_language: Russian
  collections: [banned-challenged, original-language]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: A jealous husband recounts a marriage destroyed by sexuality, resentment, and murder.
  why_included: Banned in Russia and acted against by U.S. postal authorities as allegedly obscene.
  tags: [marriage, sexuality, censorship, fiction]

- id: tess-of-the-durbervilles
  title: Tess of the d'Urbervilles
  author: Thomas Hardy
  original_year: 1891
  original_language: English
  collections: [banned-challenged, essential-literature]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: A rural woman suffers exploitation and punishment under class and sexual double standards.
  why_included: Censored by publishers for rape, sexuality, illegitimacy, religion, and criticism of Victorian morality.
  tags: [gender, class, religion, fiction]

- id: jude-the-obscure
  title: Jude the Obscure
  author: Thomas Hardy
  original_year: 1895
  original_language: English
  collections: [banned-challenged, essential-literature]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: A working-class scholar's ambitions and unconventional relationship collide with marriage law and social judgment.
  why_included: Denounced and burned for sexuality, religion, marriage, suicide, and social criticism.
  tags: [class, marriage, religion, fiction]

- id: dracula
  title: Dracula
  author: Bram Stoker
  original_year: 1897
  original_language: English
  collections: [banned-challenged, essential-literature]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: A group confronts an invasive vampire whose power mixes sexuality, blood, disease, and supernatural terror.
  why_included: Challenged for sexuality, violence, occult material, blood, and religious objections.
  tags: [gothic, horror, sexuality, fiction]

- id: the-turn-of-the-screw
  title: The Turn of the Screw
  author: Henry James
  original_year: 1898
  original_language: English
  collections: [banned-challenged, essential-literature]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: A governess believes two children are influenced by ghosts, though her reliability remains uncertain.
  why_included: Challenged for sexuality, child endangerment, psychological disturbance, and supernatural content.
  tags: [gothic, psychology, horror, fiction]

- id: the-age-of-innocence
  title: The Age of Innocence
  author: Edith Wharton
  original_year: 1920
  original_language: English
  collections: [banned-challenged, essential-literature]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: Desire and duty conflict within elite nineteenth-century New York society.
  why_included: Challenged for adultery, sexuality, and criticism of marriage and social conformity.
  tags: [marriage, class, literary-canon, fiction]

- id: we
  title: We
  original_title: My
  author: Yevgeny Zamyatin
  original_year: 1921
  original_language: Russian
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge, essential-literature, original-language]
  rights_status: historical-work
  catalog_status: source-linked
  description: A mathematician in a totalitarian glass city develops imagination, desire, and rebellion.
  why_included: Banned in the Soviet Union and foundational to dystopian fiction.
  tags: [authoritarianism, dystopia, surveillance, fiction]

  }
}
